symptoms.name: post-operative pain
symptoms.severity: currently mild, attributed by patient to the medication (turn 4)

relevant_history: Day 1 post laparoscopic appendectomy for acute appendicitis (patient context)
On combined oral contraceptive pill (patient context, raised turn 8)
Student with exams in 2 weeks (stated turn 2)

vitals.status: not measured during the conversation; surgeon happy for discharge (turn 1)

diagnosis.primary: Acute appendicitis, post laparoscopic appendectomy (patient context / surgeon happy to discharge)

prescribed_medication.notes: take regularly for the first few days (turn 5)

prescribed_medication.frequency: as needed for breakthrough pain only

follow_up: Telephone follow-up with the surgical team in 2 weeks (stated turn 9)
Dissolvable stitches - no removal needed; remove dressings after 5 days (stated turn 3)
Return to light activity/study in about a week; avoid heavy lifting and strenuous exercise for 2-3 weeks (stated turn 3)
warning_signs_safety_netting: Wound becoming red, swollen, or leaking fluid
Abdominal pain getting worse rather than better
Unable to pass wind or open bowels for several days
patient_education: Keep wounds clean and dry for 48 hours, then shower and pat dry; dressings off after 5 days (turn 3)
Take pain relief regularly for the first few days (turn 5)
Antibiotic does not reduce the pill's effectiveness, but vomiting within ~2 hours of the pill may - use condoms as backup if vomiting (turn 9)
Teach-back done: patient correctly repeated the warning signs and wound-care advice (turn 10)

clinical_note.summary: 23F, day 1 post laparoscopic appendectomy for acute appendicitis (per context). Surgeon happy to discharge. Wound care, graded return to activity, analgesia plan and warning signs provided. Pill/antibiotic counselling given.
clinical_note.discharge_medications: Paracetamol, ibuprofen, short course of codeine PRN, post-operative antibiotic course.
clinical_note.follow_up: Telephone review with surgical team in 2 weeks. Return advice given.
clinical_note.teach_back: Patient accurately repeated the warning signs and wound-care instructions.
